title: Mellowly Pungent Moroccan Pork Belly Msemen
description: A tender and romantic Moroccan dish featuring pork belly slices, shio-koji-cure, and wrapped in msemen, perfect for a special dinner.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a small bowl, mix together shio-koji, olive oil, onion, garlic, ginger, cumin, coriander, and paprika.
Place the pork belly in a large dish and brush the shio-koji mixture evenly over both sides of the pork.
Cover the dish with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Remove the pork from the refrigerator and let it sit at room temperature for 30 minutes.
Roll out the msemen dough to a large rectangle, about 1/4 inch thick.
Slice the pork belly into thin strips and place them in the center of the msemen dough.
Fold the msemen dough over the pork belly to form a triangle or square shape, and press the edges together to seal.
Place the msemen on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and bake for 25 minutes.
Flip the msemen over and bake for an additional 10 minutes, or until crispy and golden brown.
